t_ch: 为了免费盒饭！
t_en: For free meals!

t_ch: 苏乐达？两瓶糖浆，就让你们心甘情愿地为恶魔卖命？你们身为怪物的尊严到哪儿去了？
t_en: Seriously? SoulGlad and two bottles of syrup, and you're all in for the devil? What happened to your pride as monsters?

t_ch: 我都当群演了，哪儿还顾得上脸面？
t_en: I'm a background extra. Who has time for pride?

t_ch: 人、妖精，哪怕是山林中的魔怪，凡是具有灵智的生命，就拥有用双手换取希望的资格。而你们灵魂的重量，要远高于这位雇主给出的价格。
t_en: Human or monster, beast or demon — if you've got a mind of your own, then you've got the right to chase hope with your own two hands. Your soul's worth is far more than the pittance your employer is offering.

t_ch: 我无法允诺你们一个天堂。但我可以保证，我会永远站在最前面身先士卒，而非躲在背后看你们送死。
t_en: I can't promise you paradise. But I swear I'll always fight on the front lines, not hide behind you while you die.

t_ch: 是被它操控做一辈子的丑角，还是为了尊严做一瞬的骑士？现在回头还来得及。
t_en: So what'll it be? Spend your life as a dancing puppet for him, or live one moment as a knight? It's not too late to turn back now.

t_ch: 答案只有一个。
t_en: There's only one answer to that.

t_ch: 明明是我先来的，为什么你能操纵我的使魔？
t_en: I was here first! How are you controlling my familiars!?

t_ch: 「骑乘」技能的含金量。
t_en: That's what the "Riding Skill" is for!

t_ch: 领袖气质，朋友。
t_en: Call it leader charisma, my friend.

t_ch: 我们的技能优先级更高。
t_en: Our abilities prioritize over yours.

t_ch: 还有你们，听她说了两句话就叛变了？区区一群群演，工资还想不想要了？
t_en: You traitors! One pep talk and you're switching sides? You're just extras! Don't you want your paycheck?

t_ch: 你这耍大牌的家伙，除了用钱要挟我们还会做什么？我现在是骑士王的坐骑，而不是被你当狗使唤的使魔！
t_en: Look at you, acting all high and mighty! What else can you even do besides throwing money around to strong-arm us? I'm the King of Knights' mount now, not some familiar you can boss around like a dog!

t_ch: 拉姆瑞三世，我将爱马的名字赐予你。现在，随我一同讨伐眼前的邪魔！
t_en: I bestow upon you the name of my beloved steed, Llamrei III. Now, come with me — let us vanquish the evil before us!

t_ch: 以王的名义！
t_en: In the name of the king!

t_ch: 住手，如果杀了我，身为使魔的你们也会消失。
t_en: Wait! If you kill me, you all vanish too! You're still my familiars!

t_ch: 啊——啊！
t_en: Ahhh!

t_ch: 吾王剑锋所指，吾等心之所向！
t_en: Wheresoever my king's sword points, our hearts shall follow!

t_ch: 不仅没能保护御主，被迫分散，甚至还要敌人来亲自营救我，我——
t_en: I failed to protect my Master, was forced to separate from her... and now, to make matters worse, it's the enemy who came to save me. I—I am...

t_ch: 女士，这种时候，应该要说「谢谢你」，而不是「抱歉」这种丧气话。
t_en: Ma'am, this is where you say "thank you" instead of "sorry."

t_ch: 可再怎么辩解， 也无法掩盖我的无能和失职。没有力量的从者，不过是个毫无价值的玩偶。
t_en: But no excuse can cover up my incompetence and negligence. A powerless Servant is nothing but a worthless doll.

t_ch: 关于知更鸟小姐，有件事需要——算了，你们还是直接跟我来吧。
t_en: About Ms. Robin, there's something... never mind. Just come with me.

t_ch: 不知道敌人做了什么，知更鸟小姐她分裂成了好多的…我花了不少时间，才找到眼前的这些。
t_en: I'm not sure what the enemy did, but she's been split into pieces... Took me a while to find even these few.

t_ch: 果然，我在这场战争中根本一事无成，不配被称作英灵。
t_en: I figured I've managed to do nothing in this war. I don't deserve to be called a Heroic Spirit.

t_ch: 你已经做得很好了。
t_en: You have done extremely well.

t_ch: 还是葛瑞迪太卑鄙。
t_en: It's Grady who plays dirty.

t_ch: 梦回不要笑挑战。
t_en: This kinda reminds me of the try-not-to-laugh challenge.

t_ch: 喜欢用同谐的力量给我找茬？那就把你们的歌者分离成一团散沙，看还有谁能帮你们走出我的噩梦乐园。
t_en: Love using your little Harmony tricks on me, huh? Then I'll scatter your singer across my nightmare park and see who's helping you to get out of here.

t_ch: ♪请不要～因我们而～～感伤♪。
t_en: ♪ Please don't~ be sad because of us~ ♪

t_ch: ♪你已～做到了最好～～啊～～～恰似水畔的鸢尾花！♪
t_en: ♪ You've already~ done your~ best~~ Just like an iris by the riverside! ♪

t_ch: 无能就是无能，不需要为她找借口。
t_en: Call it what you want — she's still too weak. No need to sugarcoat it.

t_ch: 它忆域迷因的，等我找到奥帝那狗东西，绝对要把他的绒球薅到地上！
t_en: That old fool. Once I find that darn Oti, I'm gonna tear his puffball and stomp it right down to the ground!

t_ch: 据我判断，将我们分离成不同的个体，多半是出自报复情绪。但由于意识本身具备的自我合并特性，理论上，只要集齐所有的「我」……
t_en: Judging from what I can tell, splitting us up like this was probably an act of revenge. But our minds naturally try to reconnect. Theoretically, if we can reunite all the "me"s...

t_ch: 就能召唤同谐星神？
t_en: We summon the Aeon of Harmony?

t_ch: 饶了我吧，这里的人就没一个正经的吗？
t_en: Give me a break! Are there no functioning adults around here?

t_ch: 就能合体成巨大机器人！
t_en: You combine into a giant robot!

t_ch: 饶了我吧，这里的人就没一个正经的吗？
t_en: Give me a break! Are there no functioning adults around here?

t_ch: 就能变回原样。
t_en: You'll go back to normal.

t_ch: 正确答案是，就能聚合成原本的形态。只要你们能从恶魔们的诅咒中活下来。
t_en: The correct answer is: She'll merge back into her original form. That is... if you survive the curses from the devils.

t_ch: 那我们赶紧出发吧。
t_en: Then let's get going.

t_ch: 你难道想带着一群累赘和敌人交战？先说好，掌管「行动」的那部分知更鸟没在我身上，我只负责想，不负责做。
t_en: Wait, you're really planning to drag all these deadweights with you while you fight? Heads up: I don't have the "Action" part of Robin with me. I only think. I don't do.

t_ch: 等等，请各位务必留一个人下来，与我一同保护知更鸟小姐。我知道这个要求非常过分，我可以把自己的命交给——
t_en: Wait, please, someone must stay behind with me to protect Miss Robin. I know this is asking too much, but I'll even offer my life—

t_ch: 用胁迫得来的胜利，对战士是莫大的耻辱。既然知更鸟小姐是我们的盟友，保护她的安全也是天经地义。
t_en: Victory gained through coercion is a disgrace to any warrior. Since Miss Robin is our ally, protecting her is only right.

t_ch: 我留下来好了。
t_en: I'll stay.

t_ch: 作为御主，你对魔术的抗性要远低于我们。如果你被葛瑞迪袭击退场，我也会跟着消失，这显然是敌人希望看到的。
t_en: As a Master, your resistance to magecraft is far weaker than ours. If Grady knocks you out, I disappear with you, and that's exactly what the enemy wants.

t_ch: 让Archer留下来。
t_en: Let Archer stay.

t_ch: 抱歉，{F#小姑娘}{M#小子}，我可不会听从你的指挥。我的御主目前还下落不明，没道理留在这儿帮你们照看这个裂成几瓣的孩子。
t_en: Sorry, {F#miss}{M#mister}, I don't take orders from you. My Master is still missing, so I've got no reason to babysit this shattered kid for you.

t_ch: 那就让Saber留下来。
t_en: Then let Saber stay.

t_ch: 这的确是眼下的最优解了。Archer，请务必保护好{NICKNAME}，这是我作为对手的请求。
t_en: This is indeed the best solution at the moment. Archer, please protect {NICKNAME}. This is all I ask as an opponent.

t_ch: 哼，你可不要对我有太多信心。
t_en: Hmph. Don't place too much faith in me.

t_ch: 我向来对每个朋友都很有信心——御主，祝你顺利。
t_en: I always have faith in every friend of mine — Master, good luck.

t_ch: 那么，就让骑士们留在这里保护公主，我们去收割敌人的小命好了。
t_en: Alright, let the knight stay here with the princess, and we'll go reap some enemy lives.

t_ch: 呱呱！恶灵！为何你如此憎恶人类？难道你的心中一点爱和善良都没有呱？
t_en: *ribbit* Evil spirit! Why do you despise humans so? Is there not a single ounce of love or compassion in your heart? *ribbit*

t_ch: 哼哼哼哼哼，背叛者没有资格对我们指手画脚。
t_en: Heh heh heh heh heh... traitors don't get to boss us around.

t_ch: 在失去耐心以前，我还可以给你一个弃明投暗的机会。杀死你身边的家伙，回到我们身边。
t_en: Before I lose my patience, here's your chance to switch sides. Kill the one next to you and return to us.

t_ch: 虽然形象粗制滥造，但从名字可以推断，犬形的怪物应该是《钟表小子》动画的经典形象，狼人哈努兄弟的翻版。
t_en: Despite its rough design, the dog-shaped monster's name suggests it's modeled after a classic figure from the Clockie cartoon, likely a derivative of the werewolf Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 别再硬撑了，斯科特先生！它的目标是我，没必要白白牺牲你的性命啾。
t_en: Stop pushing yourself, Mr. Skott! Its target is me. You don't have to throw your life away for nothing!

t_ch: 如果我在这里退缩了，和其他斯科特还有什么分别！想要伤害知更鸟小姐…除非，从我的尸体上跨过去呱！
t_en: If I back down now, I'd be the same as all the other Skotts. So... If you want to hurt Miss Robin... You'll have to walk over my dead body! *ribbit*

t_ch: 我眼睛出问题了？
t_en: Are my eyes playing tricks on me?

t_ch: 你和正义哪点搭边？
t_en: What do you even have to do with justice?

t_ch: 这噩梦太可怕了。
t_en: This nightmare is terrifying.

t_ch: 老对手，你来得正是时候！快帮我一把，打败这该死的恶灵。
t_en: Old foe, you've arrived just in time! Help me! Together we can crush this blasted evil spirit.

t_ch: 解释也好，惩罚也罢，只要能救下知更鸟小姐，一切都任你处置呱。
t_en: Doesn't matter if you wanna punish me later or demand an explanation... as long as Miss Robin is safe, you can do whatever you want with me. *ribbit*

t_ch: 无论来一个还是两个，结果都是一样的。你们将被困在生不如死的循环里，{RUBY_B#孤狼兄弟掌管的诅咒}玩上十亿年的《小小哈努行动》{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: One or two... it makes no difference. You'll get stuck in an endless cycle of suffering and {RUBY_B#Lonewolf Brothers' Curse}play Hanu's Adventure for the next billion years{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 孤狼兄弟发出了一声非常惬意的*哼*。
t_en: The Lonewolf Brothers let out a very satisfied *hmph*.

t_ch: 废话少说，来感受匹诺康尼最恐怖的折磨吧！
t_en: Enough chit-chat. Time for the most terrifying torment in all of Penacony!

t_ch: 刺眼的光芒里，你被强行缩小，塞进了哈努兄弟的轮廓里。
t_en: With a blinding flash, your body shrinks, and you're forced into the outline of Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 一辈子都只能玩小小哈努行动的恐惧，就用你的身体好好体会。
t_en: Now you'll know the horror of being trapped in Hanu's Adventure... for life.

t_ch: 嚯，竟然把人变成了卡通动物？是古希腊神话时代的魔术吗？不…原理似乎完全不同。
t_en: Ho! They turned people into cartoon animals? Is this some magecraft from the age of the Ancient Greek gods? No... the mechanics feel entirely different.

t_ch: 救命啊！
t_en: Help!

t_ch: 不会又只能说*哼*吧？
t_en: Don't tell me all I can say is *hmph* again?

t_ch: 我的火箭筒呢？
t_en: Where's my rocket launcher?

t_ch: 我知道你有很多话想说，但为了不破坏小小哈努行动的设定，你只能发出一声非常急切的*哼*。
t_en: I know you have a lot to say, but to preserve the integrity of Hanu's Adventure, all you can do is let out a very urgent *hmph*.

t_ch: …连沟通的理性也失去了？要不直接丢下{F#她}{M#他}算了，反正这家伙也派不上多大用场。
t_en: ...They even lost the ability to speak rationally? Maybe I should just leave {F#her}{M#him} — it's not like {F#she}{M#he} was all that helpful anyway.

t_ch: 你发出一声恐惧的*哼*。
t_en: You let out a terrified *hmph*.

t_ch: 这不是能听懂么。啧，也不能放着不管——你们能听懂{F#她}{M#他}在说什么吗？
t_en: So you can understand that. Tch, guess I can't just leave {F#her}{M#him}... Can you make sense of what {F#she}{M#he}'s saying?

t_ch: 当然，哈努兄弟说，他要一拳把恶灵给揍趴下啾啾！
t_en: Of course! Brother Hanu says he's gonna punch that evil spirit right in the face!

t_ch: 呱？
t_en: Ribbit?

t_ch: 事情为什么会变成这样…这里是托儿所还是动物园？
t_en: How did it come to this... is this a nursery or a zoo?

t_ch: 在动物们的环绕音叫声里，Archer的太阳穴以微不足道的幅度轻轻跳动，自这场圣杯战争开始以来，这是他受过的最严重的精神攻击。
t_en: Surrounded by animal echoing cries, a vein throbbed faintly on Archer's temple. It was the worst mental trauma he'd suffered since the Holy Grail War began.

t_ch: 你很想告诉Archer，自己急需一台<color=#dbc291ff>哈努电视机</color>来变回原型。要不在周围找找？或许有能给他提示的东西。
t_en: You really want to tell Archer you need a <color=#dbc291ff>Hanu TV</color> to return to normal. Maybe you should look around for clues?

t_ch: 你以为能靠这台电视机变回去？但孤狼兄弟已经早你一步，破坏了影视乐园里所有的哈努电视机。
t_en: Think you can turn back using a TV? The Lonewolf Brothers already smashed every Hanu TV in the theme park ahead of time.

t_ch: 怎么，这台电视机有什么蹊跷吗？还是说，解开诅咒的方法和它有关？
t_en: What, is there something weird about this TV? Or is it part of the solution to the curse?

t_ch: 发出肯定的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a confirming *hmph*.

t_ch: 发出沮丧的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a dejected *hmph*.

t_ch: 发出困惑的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a confused *hmph*.

t_ch: 你抱着不切实际的希望看向一旁的斯科特，但作为一台正义的电视机，他只能为你提供除帮助以外的一切支持。
t_en: Clinging to the smallest of hopes, you glance at Skott. But as a righteous TV, all he can offer is anything but help.

t_ch: 或许是心理作用，你从他的表情里看出来三分凉薄和七分讥讽。带着一肚子火气，你狠狠地踢了斯科特一脚。
t_en: Maybe it's just your imagination, but his expression seems... <unbreak>30%</unbreak> smug, <unbreak>70%</unbreak> mocking. You lash out, kicking Skott in frustration.

t_ch: 呱？
t_en: Ribbit?

t_ch: 这样吧，我每问你一个问题，如果回答「是」，你就踢这家伙一脚。如果「不是」，你就踢两脚。
t_en: Alright, let's do this: Every time I ask you a question, and if the answer's yes, kick him once. If it's no, kick twice.

t_ch: 这台电视机是解开诅咒的关键，对吗？
t_en: This TV is the key to lifting the curse, isn't it?

t_ch: 对，踢一脚。
t_en: Yes. Kick once.

t_ch: 呱？
t_en: Ribbit?

t_ch: 依照一般魔术的逻辑，只要破坏掉核心，就能破除效果。你需要我打碎这个电视机吗？
t_en: By usual magecraft logic, destroying the core should nullify the effect. Should I smash the TV?

t_ch: 不，踢两脚。
t_en: No. Kick twice.

t_ch: 呱呱！
t_en: *ribbit ribbit*

t_ch: 不，多踢几脚。
t_en: No. Give him a few more kicks.

t_ch: 呱呱！呱呱！
t_en: *ribbit ribbit*

t_ch: 那么反过来思考，你需要我修复这个机器，或者还原它的过程，对吧？
t_en: Then let's think in reverse. You want me to repair this machine or restore it to its original state, right?

t_ch: 对！疯狂地踢。
t_en: Yes! Kick like crazy.

t_ch: 呱呱呱呱呱呱呱呱呱呱呱。
t_en: *ribbit ribbit* *ribbit ribbit* *ribbit ribbit*

t_ch: 终于该结束了——trace on。
t_en: Time to end this — Trace on.

t_ch: 变回原样！
t_en: Back to normal!

t_ch: 不可能！你们居然找到了破解诅咒的方法！这是作弊！不算数，再来一局！
t_en: Impossible! You actually found a way to break the curse!? That's cheating! Doesn't count! I demand a rematch!

t_ch: 相同的招式，你以为自己会有第二次机会吗……
t_en: You think you'll get a second chance with the same trick...?

t_ch: 哼，看来用不着我出手了。
t_en: Hmph, looks like I won't have to step in after all.

t_ch: 谢啦伙计！这玩意儿的狗头我们可就抢下喽。
t_en: Thanks, pal! We'll be taking this thing's head now.

t_ch: 任何时间！任何地点！
t_en: Any time! Any place!

t_ch: 巡海游侠！除恶惩奸！
t_en: Galaxy Ranger! Serving justice!

t_ch: 受死吧，小宝贝！
t_en: Die, you son of a fork!

t_ch: 好久不见，虽然也没分开多一会儿。自打被那个小可爱吸进电视里，我就一直在酒店中迷路，顺便一路爱爱那些小可爱。
t_en: Long time no see... Though it's only been a little while. Ever since that shirthead sucked me into the TV, I've been lost in the hotel, sending my greetings to all the other lil' fudgepies along the way.

t_ch: 就在不久前，整个空间天塌地陷，重新站稳的时候，身边突然就多了…一位知更鸟小姐！
t_en: Just a moment ago, the whole space cracked and collapsed. When I regained my footing, bam, there was Miss Robin right next to me!

t_ch: 也是没想到，姐们儿还真有点儿游侠潜质在身上——说起来，你们边上这鼻行兽养的可爱玩意儿是什么东西？俘虏？
t_en: Didn't expect it, but the girl's got a bit of that ranger vibe in her. By the way, what's that fudgin' thing raised by the Nosewalker next to you? A prisoner?

t_ch: 呱，是一名正义的伙伴呱。
t_en: *ribbit* Here comes a hero of justice. *ribbit*

t_ch: 哈？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 也该聊聊正事了。
t_en: Alright, let's get down to business.

t_ch: 关于正义斯科特…
t_en: About Skott of Justice...

t_ch: 视情况看怎么揍你。
t_en: Depending on how this goes, I'll decide how hard to beat you up.

t_ch: 在先前的战斗里，葛瑞迪把我的意识分成了无数份，就像现在的知更鸟小姐。
t_en: Grady split my consciousness into countless fragments in the previous battle, just like what happened to Miss Robin.

t_ch: 说实话，我也感到不可思议，「斯科特」这个存在的内部，居然留有一丝「正义」？但我无法否认，我心中始终燃烧着义愤之情。
t_en: I'm honestly as shocked as you are. Who'd have thought there's "justice" in that man called Skott? And yet, my chest burns with righteous fire.

t_ch: 正义斯科特先生和那些坏斯科特不一样，他舍身保护了我啾。
t_en: Skott of Justice's not like those evil ones. He threw himself in to protect me, tweet!

t_ch: 你信这鼻行兽养的玩意儿，不如信我是仙舟的将军。别怪我说话难听，让他这样的人悔改，不如叫反物质军团去盗贼公国植树造林。
t_en: You trust this son of a Nosewalker? Might as well believe I'm a general of the Xianzhou. No offense, but making someone like him turn over a new leaf is like asking the Antimatter Legion to plant trees in the Kingdom of Banditry.

t_ch: 关于这点，我举双手赞同。我们无法承担可能的风险，如果敌人在他的身上施加了诅咒，或者将他做成特殊的使魔，万一……
t_en: I completely agree. We can't afford the risk. If the enemy cursed him or turned him into some kind of special familiar, then...

t_ch: 万一这也是斯科特的阴谋呢？如果邪恶的他只是在我的身体里沉睡了，随时准备醒来……
t_en: Then I thought... what if this is all part of Skott's plan? What if the evil side of him is lying in wait within me, ready to spring to action at any moment...

t_ch: 我想和你们同行，救下其他的知更鸟小姐。为了防范斯科特的诡计，我也需要有人能及时杀死我。
t_en: I want to go with you to save the other Miss Robins, and I also need someone to kill me if Skott ever takes over my body.

t_ch: 你接着演，看我信不信。
t_en: Keep up your act. Let's see if I fall for it.

t_ch: 我会时刻盯着你的！
t_en: I'll keep my eyes on you at all times.

t_ch: 也别为难他了，我们的时间可经不起浪费。有句老话说得好，「每只扑满都值得第二口饼干。」
t_en: Let's not be too hard on him. We don't have time to waste. As the old saying goes, "Every Trotter deserves a second cookie."

t_ch: 不如暂且收下他。若是能改邪归正，那固然好，如果不能，也能把它当作投掷武器对付他的从者。
t_en: How about we take him in for now? If he turns good, great. If not, we can always chuck him at his Servant as a projectile.

t_ch: 既然受害者都这么发话了……
t_en: Well, since the victim herself said so...

t_ch: 为了不辜负大家的信任，我也不会向邪恶屈服的呱。
t_en: To honor the trust placed in me, I will never bend to evil. *ribbit*

t_ch: 游侠女士，就麻烦你护送这只…小鸟回去和Saber她们会合，我们尽量速战速决，找到剩余的知更鸟和我那不靠谱的御主。
t_en: Miss Ranger, we'll count on you to escort this... little bird back to Saber and the others. We'll wrap this up fast, find the rest of Miss Robin, and track down my unreliable Master.

t_ch: 呱！我会用行动向你们证明我的正义呱！
t_en: *ribbit* I'll prove my justice with my actions. *ribbit*

t_ch: 在审判你们之前，有颗不听话的棋子需要优先处理——斯科特，如此卑鄙无耻的你，竟会站到他们那边。
t_en: Before I judge you all, I must first deal with a disobedient pawn — Skott, how utterly despicable that you'd side with them.

t_ch: 你就这么想当正义的伙伴吗？
t_en: So desperate to play the hero, aren't you?

t_ch: 嘶，突然感觉身体一阵恶寒。
t_en: Tch, just felt an ominous chill run down my spine.

t_ch: 跟它废话那么多干什么？一枪爱死它，什么都搞定了。
t_en: Why waste your breath on it? One bullet to fudge them up, and we're done here!

t_ch: 滴答，以为我会乖乖陪你们玩勇者游戏？葛瑞迪先生给了我一颗必中的作弊子弹，只要扣下扳机，这场戏里台词最多的御主就会当场报废。
t_en: Tick-tock, thought I'd just play along in your little hero fantasy? Mr. Grady gave me a special bullet that never misses. One pull of this trigger, and the Master with the most dialogue will drop dead on the spot.

t_ch: 受死吧！{NICKNAME}！
t_en: DIE, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 斯科特替你挡住了致命一击。
t_en: Skott took the fatal hit meant for you.

t_ch: 各位，看来我不能陪你们走下去了！但我很庆幸，我是为了正义，为了保护他人而死的呱。
t_en: I... can't keep going with all of you... But I'll die happy... knowing that I gave my life for justice... to keep others safe...

t_ch: 斯科特，你玩真的？
t_en: Skott, are you serious?

t_ch: 好死！开香槟喽！
t_en: Yay! Time to crack open the champagne!

t_ch: 你不要死哇！
t_en: Don't you dare die on me!

t_ch: 替我转告拉克什米，和她分手的那天晚上，我对她说谎了，其实……
t_en: Tell Rakshmi... the night we broke up... I lied to her... There's...

t_ch: 其实……
t_en: There's...

t_ch: 其实根本就不存在什么正义斯科特！
t_en: There's no such thing as a Skott of Justice!

t_ch: 所谓「正义」，不过是名字的一部分。怎样？这可是现在才公布的冲击性事实呱！
t_en: That "justice" is a part of the name. It doesn't mean anything! What do you think? How would you rate this shocking reveal. *ribbit*

t_ch: 导演，快帮我疗伤啊！我好不容易把他们引到了陷阱里，你给我屁股开的这枪算怎么回事！
t_en: Director! Heal me up! I worked so hard to lead them into the trap, and now you're shooting me in the butt!?

t_ch: *匹诺康尼监狱时代的粗口*，要不是你这废物抢戏，挡住了子弹，钟表老子现在已经把{NICKNAME}解决了，你居然还敢邀功？
t_en: *Penacony profanity*, if your worthless scene-stealing self hadn't blocked that bullet, Old Clockie would've eliminated {NICKNAME} already. Who are you to have the audacity to act proud?

t_ch: 让你潜入敌人内部，在剧情高潮的时候给他们当头一棒。再看看你都做了什么？连最基本的起承转合都没有，傻子都能看出你是演的！
t_en: I sent you undercover to deliver a crushing blow at the story's climax. Instead? You couldn't even manage basic narrative structure. Even a child could tell you were putting on an act!

t_ch: 既然你的表演欲这么强。那么恭喜，斯科特先生，现在您已从演员降格为演出道具！
t_en: Since you're so desperate for attention, congratulations, Mr. Skott — you're hereby demoted from actor to stage decoration!

t_ch: 那我的屁股怎么办？
t_en: Hey, what about my butt!?

t_ch: 你根本没有屁股！
t_en: You don't even have a behind!

t_ch: 挂树上就不疼了。
t_en: Just hang it up, and the pain will be gone.

t_ch: 再来一枪就不疼了。
t_en: Just one more bullet and no more pain.

t_ch: 钟表老子，别装死了。这场戏要是拍不成，我绝不支付片酬。
t_en: Old Clockie, quit playing dead. No completed scene, no paycheck for you.

t_ch: 滴答，你什么时候产生了我在装死的错觉？我的<color=#dbc291ff>钟表把戏</color>早就已经启动了。
t_en: Tick-tock, what gave you the impression I was faking? My <color=#dbc291ff>Clockwork</color> has been ticking away this whole time.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈，真他宝贝的畅快。这种时候，就该开一瓶赫尔多林的烟熏麦芽汁。
t_en: Hahaha, that was so fudgin' satisfying! Time to break out the Heldorin's smoked malt juice to celebrate!

t_ch: 男人的内心无法抑制地泛起喜悦的情绪，飘飘欲仙。而这一切，都不过是一次普通的精神攻击。
t_en: Uncontainable joy surges through the man's heart, sending him into euphoria. And yet, this is nothing more than a common mental attack.

t_ch: 钟表匠，你看到了吗？你最引以为傲的把戏，现在也不过是我的囊中之物！
t_en: Watchmaker, witness this! Your most prized trick now belongs to me!

t_ch: 该死，不知道为什么，我居然想和这混账跳一支舞。
t_en: Darn it, for some bizarre reason, I have this sudden urge to dance with this jerk.

t_ch: 滴答，这是曾君临匹诺康尼的魔王，钟表匠发明的钟表把戏。再强大的心灵，在它的面前，都只是一张随意涂改的白纸。
t_en: Tick-tock, behold the Clockwork — invented by the Watchmaker, the Devil who once ruled Penacony. In its presence, even the strongest minds become nothing but blank pages, easily rewritten at will.

t_ch: 你的技能范围怎么这么大？
t_en: How is your power's range so ridiculously broad?

t_ch: 盗版角色的能力值，不需要考虑剧情的战力平衡。
t_en: Knockoff characters don't need to worry about power balancing in the story.

t_ch: 你用我的把戏对付我！
t_en: You dare use my own powers against me, Grady?

t_ch: 你个盗版神气什么？
t_en: What are you so proud of, you cheap imitation?

t_ch: 竟敢戏弄你的好bro波提欧，来吧，{NICKNAME}，让这盗版货见识见识真正的钟表把戏。
t_en: Messing with your bro Boothill? Big mistake! Come on, {NICKNAME}, let's show this knockoff what actual Clockwork can do.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>把波提欧的情绪调整到正常状态吧。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Please reset Boothill's emotional state back to normal.</color>

t_ch: 滴答，我是你灵魂的主治医师。
t_en: Tick-tock, I am the chief physician of your soul.

t_ch: 如果你看到这句话，说明你选错了对手。
t_en: If you're reading this, it means you chose the wrong opponent.

t_ch: 瞧瞧这是谁？一个可怜的小家伙。想要解开朋友的诅咒，却是自身难保。
t_en: Well, who do we have here? A poor little thing. Trying to save your friend from a curse when you can't even protect yourself.

t_ch: 发现自己的钟表把戏不起作用，是不是很惊喜？很意外？这是当然的。
t_en: Surprised your Clockwork is useless? Shocked? Confused? Well, of course you are.

t_ch: 你的钟表把戏的力量来源于钟表小子，而我是钟表老子，小子见了老子，自然就失灵了。
t_en: The power of your Clockwork comes from Clockie, but I am Old Clockie. When the junior meets the senior, it's only natural for you to fail.

t_ch: 现在，轮到老子教训小子的时间了。
t_en: Now, it's time for the old man to teach the kid a lesson.

t_ch: 一阵难以言喻的情绪攫住你的心灵，它并非钟表把戏四种情绪中的一种。而是一种更阴郁，更沉闷的东西，宛如无言的泥沼。
t_en: A feeling beyond words clutches at your mind — not one of Clockwork's four different emotions. This is darker, heavier, like a silent quagmire.

t_ch: 挚爱之人的离去，受最信任的人背叛，日趋迫近的死期，被杜撰出的、不应存在的情绪在心头炸开，徒留下一个漆黑的孔洞，它像死人的眼睛般凝视你——
t_en: Departed loved ones, betrayal by those you trusted most, the shadow of approaching death... These manufactured emotions burst in your heart, leaving just a black void that watches you with the empty eyes of a corpse—

t_ch: 玩够了吗？我可没时间留在这儿陪你过家家。
t_en: You finished with your little show yet? I don't have time to play pretend with you.

t_ch: 怎么可能？我明明用钟表把戏把所有人的情绪改成了绝望。
t_en: Impossible! I used Clockwork to turn everyone's emotions to pure despair!

t_ch: 无聊透顶，连台词都是经典反派的那一套，这点打打闹闹的诱导，还不如死尸上秃鹫的叫声响亮。
t_en: Ugh, how trite. Your dialogue is straight from Villains <unbreak>101</unbreak>. These pathetic attempts at manipulation are less effective than vultures squawking over a corpse.

t_ch: 兄弟，你早点儿动手不好吗？非要不说话装高手？
t_en: What took you so long? Was that whole silent-badass act really necessary?

t_ch: 摸鱼要扣绩效的。
t_en: Slacking off will cost you on your performance review.

t_ch: 没事，就扣在我御主头上吧。
t_en: Whatever, just put it on my Master's tab.

t_ch: 找到敌人的弱点了吗？
t_en: Have you figured out the enemy's weak spot yet?

t_ch: 是啊，除了能动摇情绪的能力，这家伙浑身上下都是破绽。
t_en: Yep. Besides his emotion-manipulating powers, this guy's nothing but walking weak points.

t_ch: 对魔力抵抗了精神攻击吗？
t_en: Was it your Magic Resistance that blocked the mental attack?

t_ch: 我也希望如此，可惜，我的对魔力并不像你那位Saber一样作弊。
t_en: If only it were that simple. Unfortunately, my Magic Resistance isn't nearly as overpowered as Saber's.

t_ch: 我本想看看，在这个异常的宇宙里，这怪物眼中的地狱能到什么程度。但现在看来，你们的世界似乎还有希望和出路。
t_en: I wanted to see how deep this monster's hell could go in this strange universe of yours. But it seems your world still has hope and a future after all.

t_ch: 绝望？要说绝望，我见过的绝望景象多得能淹没这座噩梦乐园。
t_en: Despair? Please. I've witnessed enough scenes of despair to submerge this entire playground of nightmare.

t_ch: 我见过尸体垂吊在绞架上，像森林般望不到尽头；我见过末日大战后的焦土，人与人堕落成饥饿的兽群彼此吞吃……
t_en: I've seen corpses hanging from gallows that stretch like endless forests. I've witnessed the charred remains after doomsday battles where people became nothing more than ravenous beasts devouring each other...

t_ch: 你…你到底是什么来头？！
t_en: Just who... who in the world ARE you!?

t_ch: 一个勤劳的守护者，只是勤劳到有些工作狂罢了。我见过的绝望很多很多…但要想吞没我，你这些虚假的幻觉还不够！
t_en: A hardworking guardian, though admittedly somewhat of a workaholic. I've seen far too many forms of despair... but to break me? Your fake illusions don't even come close!

t_ch: 滴答，把功率开到最高，我就不信还有人能在我的钟表把戏里挺住。
t_en: Tick-tock, cranking power to maximum! I refuse to accept that anyone could resist my Clockwork now!

t_ch: 你见过被战车碾压的尸骸吗？Trace on！
t_en: Ever seen bodies mangled under war chariots? Trace on!

t_ch: 不要得意，导演他会为我报仇的。我，我——
t_en: Don't get smug, the director will avenge me. I, I—

t_ch: 说再多台词也不会给你加钱的。
t_en: Extra lines won't earn you extra pay.

t_ch: 啊，我死了。
t_en: O, in that case, I'm dead.

t_ch: 好了，现在去找人会合吧。
t_en: Good, now let's regroup with the others.

t_ch: 经过短暂的休息后，四散在片场各处的盟友终于合归一处。
t_en: After a short break, the scattered allies finally reconvened from across the set.

t_ch: 没想到，竟然发生了这么多事…谢谢你，替我保护了御主。
t_en: I had no idea so much was happening... Thank you for keeping my Master safe.

t_ch: 哼，只是不想遂了葛瑞迪的愿而已。
t_en: Hmph, I just didn't want to give Grady the satisfaction.

t_ch: 在道谢之前，还有更重要的事要做吧。那小姑娘不是说，只要让所有的自己聚到一起，就能变回原样吗？
t_en: Let's save the thanks for later. Didn't that girl say if we brought all her split selves together, she could return to normal?

t_ch: 我提出这一理论的前提是，没有任何知更鸟处于异常状态。
t_en: I proposed this theory assuming none of the Robins would be in a defect status.

t_ch: 乐曲中多出了一道不协和音，绝望。钟表老子——该死，葛瑞迪，你让我说出了什么污秽的词汇！
t_en: There's a dissonance in the melody — Despair. You Old Clockie — dang it, Grady, what foul language are you having me use!?

t_ch: 总之，残存在最后一位御主身上的情绪，并没有随着敌人的死亡消失。
t_en: Anyway, the emotion affecting the last Master hasn't disappeared despite the enemy's demise.

t_ch: 难道知更鸟变不回来了？
t_en: Are you saying Robin can't change back?

t_ch: 星期日知道会杀了我的。
t_en: Sunday will absolutely kill me dead.

t_ch: 七个人会不会更强一些？
t_en: Wouldn't seven of her be more powerful anyway?

t_ch: 我的弦音远不如知更鸟小姐那样强大，但驱散这点不协和音，还是能做到的。御主，接下来请安静些，不要扰乱了我的旋律。
t_en: My resonance isn't nearly as powerful as Miss Robin's, but it's enough to clear this minor dissonance. Master, please stay quiet and don't disturb my melody.

t_ch: 不要在意你看到的东西，那些不过是我心中的疑虑，不会伤害到其他人。
t_en: Ignore whatever you see — these are just my internal doubts and won't hurt anyone else.

t_ch: 然后，重组起属于你自己的旋律。
t_en: Now, rebuild a melody that's truly your own.

t_ch: 谢谢大家的帮助，没有你们，我肯定已经被他宝贝的——抱歉，意识分离的影响似乎还没有完全痊愈。
t_en: Thank you everyone for your help. Without you, I would definitely have been fudgin'— sorry, seems the consciousness-splitting effects haven't completely worn off.

t_ch: 大家请离我近一些。我已经捋清了这部影片的旋律，只要稍作调律，应该就能离开葛瑞迪的宝具了。
t_en: Everyone, please come closer. I've decoded this film's melody. With a few adjustments, we should break free from Grady's Noble Phantasm.

t_ch: 御主，你给了我参加圣杯战争的机会，我也救了你一次。现在，我们两清了。
t_en: Master, you gave me the chance to join the Holy Grail War, and I saved you once in return. We're now square.

t_ch: 这可真是意外收获——老朋友，你终于明白了吗？到底谁才能实现你的愿望。
t_en: What a pleasant surprise — old friend, have you finally understood that who it is that can truly grant your wish?

t_ch: 一定是恶灵的影响还在作祟，让我替你进行调律——
t_en: The evil spirit must still be affecting you. Let me tune you properly—

t_ch: 御主，你是个过于善良的姑娘，对圣杯毫无欲求。所以，我必须自己踏上道路。
t_en: Master, you're far too kind-hearted, with no interest in the Holy Grail. That's why I must pursue this path myself.

t_ch: 老奥帝先生是个商人，他向你讨要的代价会相当高昂。
t_en: Mr. Oti is a businessman through and through. His price will be astronomically high.

t_ch: 那又如何？你难道会背叛自己的朋友，让我赢下圣杯战争？
t_en: And? Would you really betray your friends just to let me win the Holy Grail War?

t_ch: 给你个建议。趁我还没离开，用令咒杀死我，就像过去那些随意摆弄我的命运的家伙。
t_en: Here's some advice: While I'm still here, use a Command Spell to kill me — just like all those others who toyed with my fate.

t_ch: 我能把这看作宣战吗？既然你已经不是知更鸟小姐的从者，我也没必要放任一位潜在的敌人逃走。
t_en: Is this a declaration of war? Since you're no longer Miss Robin's Servant, I have no reason to let a potential enemy walk away.

t_ch: 我能看出你仍心存犹豫，在我拔出剑以前，还有回头的余地。
t_en: I can tell you're still hesitating. Before I draw my blade, there's still time to change your mind.

t_ch: 各位，你们是不是忘了我还在这儿呢。
t_en: Everyone, aren't you forgetting something? I'm still here!

t_ch: 我的老朋友好不容易才找到了实现愿望的道路，我可不容许有人成为她道路上的阻碍。想摆脱我的宝具？那我再就给你们加点料！
t_en: My old friend has finally found her path to fulfilling her wish, and I won't let anyone block her way. You wanna escape my Noble Phantasm? Then I'll just have to add some more excitement!

t_ch: 第二场展映已经结束，让我们在最后的噩梦里再会吧！
t_en: The second screening has ended. See you in the final nightmare!

t_ch: 再见了，御主，愿你的善良永不会遭背叛。
t_en: Goodbye, Master. May your kindness never meet betrayal.

t_ch: 导演，那我屁股呢？屁股呱！
t_en: Director! What about my butt? My butt! My—!

t_ch: 如果你做不到的话，知更鸟小姐，我们就此别过。
t_en: If this is beyond you, Miss Robin, then we must part ways here.

t_ch: 静默三幕剧•其三
t_en: The Silent Stage: Act III

t_ch: 《外太空计划六号》
t_en: Plan 6 from Outer Space

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actors

t_ch: 自欺的心
t_en: Self-deceiving Heart

t_ch: 英雄的血浆，怪物的血浆，远航之人在异乡大杀四方，这正是拓荒时代的逐梦客们最爱的东西。
t_en: Hero's blood, monster's blood... The story details how explorers cause bloodshed in strange worlds — this is exactly what Dreamchasers of the pioneering age live for.

t_ch: 英雄？逝者？在这颗星球上，它们都不过是能用苜蓿币度量的消费品。
t_en: Heroes? The dead? On this planet, they're just commodities with a price tag in Alfalfa credits.

t_ch: 唉，斯科特先生，我知道你在本部门干得不如意，但如果你能表现出足够的诚意，战略投资部或许可以给你一个更好的岗位——
t_en: Ah, Mr. Skott, I know things haven't been going smoothly for you in your department, but if you can show enough commitment, the Strategic Investment Department might have a better spot for you —

t_ch: 方才对各位的敌意，都是为了欺骗敌人不得已而为之。我特地潜伏在奥帝身边，就是为了在这一刻，为真正配得上圣杯的您献上胜利！
t_en: My earlier hostility was just an act to fool our enemies. I infiltrated Oti's group specifically for this moment — to help you win the Holy Grail, the only one who truly deserves it!

t_ch: 惊不惊喜？意不意外？
t_en: Didn't see that coming, did you?

t_ch: 只要投身老奥帝麾下，替你们所有人实现愿望，也不无可能。这个道理，最精通投资的战略投资部高管，想必能做出合格的回答。我说的对吗，砂金先生？
t_en: Just pledge yourself to Old Oti, help grant everyone's wishes, and it's quite possible. Surely the Strategic Investment Department's top executive, who understands value, can give a proper answer. Right, Mr. Aventurine?

t_ch: 你似乎对我格外有执念啊，导演？是因为我以一个外行影迷的身份反复锐评了您的杰作吗？
t_en: You seem particularly obsessed with me, Director. Is it because I, as an amateur film critic, repeatedly tore apart your masterpiece?

t_ch: 当然是因为，这些人中你最聪明，最明白「你们的力量看似强大，却始终没能摆脱我的结界」这一事实。
t_en: Of course it's because you're the cleverest one here, most aware of the fact that despite your apparent power, none of you have actually escaped my Bounded Field.

t_ch: 在你这样的人眼中，变节永远是个选择题，而不是是非题。
t_en: For someone like you, betrayal is always an option to consider, not a moral absolute.

t_ch: 我这样的人吗？…哼，你拍电影的水平不怎么样，拿捏观众的眼光倒不差。
t_en: Someone like me? ...Hmph, you may be a terrible filmmaker, but you're not bad at reading people.

t_ch: 他宝了个贝的，你该不会——
t_en: Fork me, you're not seriously—

t_ch: 成功的商人总是会审时度势，适时改变立场进行投资。我厌倦了这永无止尽的闹剧，能一次性排除这么多对手，收益倒也不坏。
t_en: A good businessman always evaluates the situation and adjusts their stance for better investment. I'm tired of this endless farce. Eliminating this many competitors at once is quite profitable.

t_ch: 砂金，你骗我？
t_en: Aventurine, you're double-crossing me?

t_ch: 你不是对圣杯不感兴趣吗？
t_en: I thought you didn't care about the Holy Grail?

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}，我说谎了。
t_en: Apologies, {NICKNAME}, I wasn't being honest.

t_ch: Great！我就喜欢你这种懂得变通的演员。那么我也该展现合作的诚意了——让我们进入下一幕吧，以你的咖位，值得一个更大的舞台。
t_en: Great! I love flexible actors like you. Now let me show my goodwill — let's move to the next scene. Your caliber deserves a much bigger stage.

t_ch: 静默三幕剧•特别加场
t_en: The Silent Stage: Special Feature

t_ch: 《金色通行魔》
t_en: The Golden Gatekeeper

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actors

t_ch: 砂金
t_en: Aventurine

t_ch: Archer，作为从者，你会站在我这一边对吧？
t_en: Archer. As my Servant, you'll come with me, right?

t_ch: 接受葛瑞迪这样的烂片导演的摆布？你认真吗？
t_en: Taking orders from a hack like Grady? Seriously?

t_ch: 也对，比起这群人，你这浑身铜臭味的家伙明显和葛瑞迪更臭味相投。
t_en: Makes sense — compared to this lot, you and Grady are clearly cut from the same money-grubbing cloth.

t_ch: 为了胜利，卑鄙些又何妨？
t_en: What's wrong with being a little underhanded if we can win?

t_ch: 你要向葛瑞迪效忠？
t_en: You're actually pledging yourself to Grady?

t_ch: 砂金，你真的背叛了吗？
t_en: Aventurine, did you really turn on us?

t_ch: 这可比斯科特难打多了。
t_en: This is going to be way tougher than Skott was.

t_ch: 啧，算我错看了你。看来，得多花几颗子弹摆平这家伙了。
t_en: Tch, totally misjudged you. Guess I'll need to use a few extra fudgin' bullets on this guy.

t_ch: 不，最后斩下敌将的，应该是我的剑才对。
t_en: No, it should be my sword that delivers the final blow to the enemy commander.

t_ch: 御主，如果你只是在开玩笑，现在道歉我还能替你解释两句。但你要是一意孤行，这可不是不流血就能结束的剧情。
t_en: Master, if you're just joking, I can still cover for you if you apologize now. But if you're serious, this won't end without blood being spilled.

t_ch: 我还是那句话。你是想先被切掉腿呢？还是先被砍下那只碍眼的手，砂、金、先、生？
t_en: I'll ask one more time. Shall I remove your legs first? Or should I start by cutting off that irritating hand of yours, Mis-ter-A-ven-tur-ine?

t_ch: 拒绝么…我本该用令咒让你屈服，但我实在讨厌这种烙在身上，能使唤别人的东西。
t_en: So you still refuse... I should use my Command Spells to force you into submission, but I hate brands on the flesh that bend someone to another's will.

t_ch: 如果站在他们身边是你的愿望，随你便好。只是有时候，人终究要换个位置，才能看清赌局的全貌。
t_en: If staying with them is your wish, then so be it. But sometimes... a change in perspective will make you see the gamble in its entirety.

t_ch: *叹气* 也是，杀了你，我就无法存在啦。
t_en: *sighs* That's right, killing you would erase my existence.

t_ch: 那么，抱歉啦，各位——我投降了，投降。
t_en: Well then, sorry everyone — I surrender, I give up.

t_ch: Archer？！
t_en: ARCHER!?

t_ch: 你投得也太快了！
t_en: You gave up WAY too quickly!

t_ch: 这下三对三了。
t_en: Now we're evenly matched, three against three.

t_ch: 干脆我们也投了吧。
t_en: Perhaps we should all surrender as well.

t_ch: 御主，哪怕是开玩笑，也请不要说出这种有悖骑士精神的话。
t_en: Master, even as a joke, please don't say things that dishonor the knight's code.

t_ch: 谁让我抽签运气这么差呢？我早说过，一个浑身金光闪闪的男人绝不会是什么好东西。
t_en: What did I expect with my awful summoning luck? I told you — a guy covered in gold is never good news.

t_ch: 但身为你的从者，我会贯彻你的意志的。
t_en: But as your Servant, I'll follow your commands.

t_ch: 太棒了，一下子多出两个举世难求的主演，我要给你们加戏！让你们成为无敌的怪物，把所有人都撕成碎片！
t_en: Fantastic! Two more exceptional leads at once! I'll give you bigger roles! Turn you into unstoppable monsters who tear everyone apart!

t_ch: 等等！导演，我也能叛变！我也想加戏！导——
t_en: Wait! Director, I can be a traitor too! I want more scenes too! Dir—

t_ch: 呱，老朋友，我会在背后支持你的呱。
t_en: *ribbit* Old friend, I'll be rooting for you.

t_ch: 加油，超酷的哈努开拓者，相信你能打败坏蛋的啾。
t_en: All the best, ultra-cool Hanu Trailblazer, I believe you can defeat that baddie, tweet.

t_ch: 别闹了，让我想想该怎么解开这个诅咒。
t_en: Enough messing around. Let me think how we can lift this curse.

t_ch: 下一把，下一把我绝对能赢回来！
t_en: Next round! I'll definitely win the next round!

t_ch: 你们先走吧，等我结束最后一局就会赶上的。
t_en: You go on ahead, I'll join you after I wrap up this last game.

t_ch: 熟识的二人似乎正在谈论一些往事，暂时还是别打扰他们吧。
t_en: The two acquaintances seem to be talking about the past. Let's not interrupt them for now.

t_ch: 砂金在替某位同僚讨要签名，究竟是谁呢？真令人好奇。
t_en: Aventurine is asking for an autograph on behalf of a colleague, who could it be? How curious.

t_ch: 接受聚光灯的照耀吧，砂金先生，进入我专为你准备的高光情节吧！
t_en: Step into the spotlight, Mr. Aventurine, and enjoy this special featurette I've crafted just for you!

t_ch: 是时候结束这场战斗了！
t_en: It's time to end this battle!

t_ch: 那么，抱歉啦，各位——
t_en: Well then, sorry everyone —

t_ch: 比起在现场到处乱跑，还是先帮帮你的老朋友波提欧吧。
t_en: Instead of running around aimlessly, maybe lend a hand to your old friend Boothill first.

t_ch: 你感受到她身上散发出强大的气息，仿佛在面对千军万马。
t_en: You can feel her aura bearing down on you, as if standing before an army.

t_ch: 虽然Archer总是嘴上不饶人，但作为同伴还是很可靠的。御主，祝你顺利。
t_en: Archer may have a sharp tongue, but when it comes down to it, he's a solid ally. Master, best of luck to you.

t_ch: …我的御主，就拜托你们了。
t_en: My Master... I'm counting on all of you.

t_ch: 知更鸟们向你投来饱含祝福的目光，虽然你根本看不见她们的眼睛。
t_en: Robins cast their blessing-filled gaze your way — not that you can actually see their eyes.

t_ch: 呦，看来你们刚刚经历了一场恶战啊。
t_en: Oh, looks like you just crawled out of a tough fight.

t_ch: 他虚空鲸的，打完了你知道出来了？
t_en: Forkin' void song whale, now you decide to appear after we did all the work?

t_ch: 我可不是什么都没干啊，有个叫左轮总督的恶灵缠着我，非要和我玩一局，把十年的存款都输给了我——瞧，我还带了个老朋友回来。
t_en: Well, I did do something, alright? Some evil spirit called the Revolver Governor got all clingy with me, insisted we play a game — and ended up losing ten years' worth of savings to me. Look, I even brought an old friend back with me.

t_ch: 呼，呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 这烂片还有完没完了？
t_en: This bargain-bin flick still isn't over?

t_ch: 又臭又长，兄弟！
t_en: Ugh, so loooong and boring!

t_ch: 退票！退票！
t_en: Refund! I want a refund!

t_ch: 匹诺康尼史上最伟大的导演免费伺候你们几个，你还敢有意见？给我安静听着！
t_en: You got the greatest director in Penacony's history giving you a show for free, and you've got complaints? Sit down and listen!

t_ch: 敌人已经被无力化了，乘胜追击吧！
t_en: The enemy's down! Now's our chance — go for the finish!

t_ch: 他宝贝的呜呜伯，有完没完！这些片子说到底不都是一回事吗？
t_en: Dadgum Wubbaboo, when will this ever end!? Aren't these movies all the same?

t_ch: 哎呀，哎呀，连牛仔这样正儿八经的拓荒者都看不下去了。我说导演，虽然我不了解你的生平…但毫无疑问，票房惨败、潦倒而死的结局八成是注定了的。
t_en: Oh. Oh my. Even a proper pioneer like the cowboy can't bear to watch any longer. Director, though I don't know your life story... but surely a box office disaster and a miserable end seem all but fated.

t_ch: 这位…斯科特先生，您站到了我们这边，是打算弃暗投明吗？看起来，Assassin拍的烂片连他的御主也忍受不了了。
t_en: And you... Mr. Skott, did you change your tune because you are planning to abandon the darkness and embrace the light? Looks like not even Assassin's Master can tolerate his trashy films anymore.

t_ch: 导演！你是不是把我放错了位置？我还在敌人堆里呢！
t_en: Director! Did you place me in the wrong spot? I'm still among the bad guys!

t_ch: 不不不，鉴于你此前「出色」的表演，我们的合作关系就此终止。至于老奥帝那边，我会替你申请一笔意外伤亡保险，发放给你的家人。
t_en: No no no, given your "stellar" performance earlier, our partnership ends here. As for Old Oti, I'll file a claim for accidental casualty compensation on your behalf. It'll be issued to your family.

t_ch: 因为，这部《外太空计划六号》的结局是——无人生还。
t_en: Because... the ending of this Plan 6 from Outer Space is — no survivors.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼呱呱呱呱，葛瑞迪！既然你不仁，也就别怪我不义了——砂～金～总～监！
t_en: Hmph, hm ahem— *cough* Grady! Since you've shown no mercy, don't blame me for returning the favor — Di~rec~tor~Aven~tu~rine~!

t_ch: 我要吐了。
t_en: I may vomit.

t_ch: 我已经麻木了。
t_en: This doesn't even faze me no more.

t_ch: 出现了，斯科特的求饶特技！
t_en: There it is. Skott's special begging technique!

t_ch: 为什么你接受得这么自然……
t_en: How do you accept such things so easily...

t_ch: 葛瑞迪，你不会以为我真的只会像个傻子一样受你摆弄吧？
t_en: Grady, you don't think I was truly going to let you play me like a fool, right?

t_ch: 作为演员，我也是你宝具的一部分！既然如此，曾是主演的我，自然也能动用你第二宝具的力量！
t_en: As an actor, I am part of your Noble Phantasm, too! As such, being the former protagonist, I can naturally use your second Noble Phantasm's power!

t_ch: 你竟敢——
t_en: How dare you...

t_ch: 砂金总监，这就是我的诚意！实装吧——<size=36><color=#dbc291ff>「员工通道，一键跳过宝具！」</color></size>
t_en: Director Aventurine, this is my show of commitment to you! Deploying — <size=36><color=#dbc291ff>"Staff Access: One-Click Noble Phantasm Skip!"</color></size>

t_ch: 非常抱歉，播映发生故障
t_en: Apologies. The broadcast is having technical difficulties

t_ch: 进度条正在飞速快进
t_en: Progress bar is progressing rapidly

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actors

t_ch: 都别演了
t_en: Everyone, please stop acting

t_ch: 林登•斯科特！我*监狱星时代粗口*，怎么会有这么没有素质的观众！
t_en: Lyndon Skott! Well, *obscene language from the prison world era* me. How can there be an audience so uncultured!

t_ch: 电影的精华都被跳过了！这可是我投入了毕生心血的——
t_en: You skipped all the best parts of the movie! This is the result of my entire life's work—

t_ch: ——烂片。
t_en: —And it was trash.

t_ch: 你什么都不懂！我、我是梦泡电影的创造者，是我建立了匹诺康尼的第一家影视公司！如果不是我放弃了对电影技术的专利垄断，钟表匠和梅芙恩都不可能有后来的成就！
t_en: You know nothing! I—I am the creator of the dream bubble movie genre. It was I who established the first film company in Penacony! If I hadn't given up on my monopoly on film-making technology, the Watchmaker and Maeven would never have achieved what they did!

t_ch: 所以呢？你拍摄的这些，难道就不是烂片了？
t_en: So? Would that make the films you made... not bad?

t_ch: 我甚至有些怜悯你，在真正的天才面前一败涂地的，导演葛瑞迪。
t_en: I'm even feeling a little bad for you now, Director Grady. You were thoroughly outclassed by true auteurs.

t_ch: 作为营销大师或发明家，你无疑是成功的。但{RUBY_B#我们}观众{RUBY_E#}并不关心你付出了什么，他们只知道，你的电影真的很烂。
t_en: As a master of marketing or an inventor, you're undoubtedly a success. But {RUBY_B#we}the audience{RUBY_E#} doesn't care what you gave in your efforts, they just know that your movie is terrible.

t_ch: 唉，你感受不到{RUBY_B#我们}观众{RUBY_E#}耐心的极限…只是用无休无止、烂俗到爆、没有声音、连内容都不再刺激的血浆片情节浪费大家的时间。
t_en: *sigh* You can't feel the limits of {RUBY_B#our}the audience's{RUBY_E#} patience... It's just an endless, cliché-ridden, silent series of utterly unexciting gore. What a waste of everyone's time.

t_ch: 也难怪，这样的你最终会被观众抛弃啊。
t_en: It's no surprise that the audience eventually abandoned you.

t_ch: 呵呵…呵呵哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Heh... Hahahahaha...

t_ch: 完了，没救了，这家伙多半是疯了。
t_en: Well, it's hopeless. This guy's probably gone mad.

t_ch: Archer……
t_en: Archer...

t_ch: 干嘛？我只是补了最后一箭而已，让他发疯的可是你啊。
t_en: What? I simply offered him a final shot. You're the one who drove him insane.

t_ch: 好一个咄咄逼人、有趣、张狂的演员！难得有人能将我驳斥到哑口无言……
t_en: What a sharp-tongued, amusing, and flamboyant actor you are! It's rare for anyone to make me go completely speechless during a debate...

t_ch: 把你们闷死在这噩梦默剧的故事里未免无趣了。说起来，你有兴趣站在老奥帝这边吗？
t_en: Strangling you all in this nightmare pantomime feels a bit dull now. Tell me, would you be interested in siding with Old Oti?

t_ch: 这又是唱的哪出？挑拨离间？
t_en: What are you yapping about this time? Tryin' to drive a wedge?

t_ch: 与我联手，一下子便能将这些对手排除在外。这个道理，最精通投资的战略投资部高管，想必能做出合格的回答。我说的对吗，砂金先生？
t_en: If we join forces, we could sweep the rest of these opponents off the board in an instant. Surely, a senior executive of the Strategic Investment Department who understands investments better than anyone would make the right choice. Wouldn't you agree, Mr. Aventurine?

t_ch: 为什么恐怖片里，演员总是看不见怪物从何而来？只要电影仍在继续，就一定会有新的怪物跳入镜头。
t_en: Why do actors in horror films never see where the monsters come from? Why do monsters keep popping into the frame as long as the film is rolling?

t_ch: 因为，真正的怪物诞生自片场之外，也即——导演的镜头。
t_en: It's because the true monster is born beyond the set. The director's lens... is the real cradle of terrors.

t_ch: 你的「气息遮断」，不过是因为「电影中的角色无法察觉到镜头外的拍摄者」而已。
t_en: Your Presence Concealment skill is simply a manifestation of the theatrical rule that characters in the story cannot see the cinematographer filming behind the lens.

t_ch: 而怪物是恐怖片的灵魂，是镜头的焦点，它们的目光永远正对着暴露的机位。所以，扮演怪物的斯科特才能看见你。
t_en: But monsters are different. They are the soul of horror films, the focus of the lens. Their line of sight is always fixed on the camera's position. That's why only Skott, the man playing the monster... can see you.

t_ch: 我输了，输得心服口服。
t_en: You win... I concede.

t_ch: 魔鬼藏在镜头之外，让真正的怪物穿过荧幕，走进影院，这是我最得意的一场线下营销。没想到在几百年后，还有人能看穿我的把戏。
t_en: Having the devil lurking behind the lens and getting real monsters to step into the theater, that was my finest offline marketing campaign ever. Who'd have thought that someone could still see through my tricks hundreds of years in the future?

t_ch: 你是怎么做到的，朋友？
t_en: How did you do it, friend?

t_ch: 我没有说过吗？葛瑞迪先生，我是你的粉丝来着。
t_en: Did I never mention it to you? I'm your fan, Mr. Grady.

t_ch: 我对你的品味不做评价。
t_en: I reserve judgment on your film taste.

t_ch: 难怪你这么熟悉他的电影。
t_en: No wonder you know his films so well.

t_ch: 不是，你之前这么锐评他…
t_en: Wait, weren't you the one who roasted him so hard before?

t_ch: 一个死了几百年的烂片导演，能让战略投资部的大人物看上？感谢你的安慰，但你显然不太会撒谎。
t_en: A director of flops from centuries ago catching the eye of a big shot from the Strategic Investment Department? Thanks for the compliment, but that was a pretty bad lie from you.

t_ch: 对自己自信些吧，导演先生。在偶然看过你的第一部电影后，出于好奇，我曾经翻阅过葛瑞迪影业的历史资料。
t_en: Have more confidence in yourself, Mr. Grady. After I stumbled upon your first movie, curiosity led me to dig through the archives of Grady Films.

t_ch: 「在进影院前和观众签生死状」、「让真正的忆域迷因在恐怖片场中进入影厅」…这些互动和营销手段，完全超越了所处的时代！
t_en: Now, you had each moviegoer sign a death and liability release waiver before entering the theater... And you released Memory Zone Memes into the theater at intermission... These interactive marketing tactics were way ahead of their time.

t_ch: 能敏锐地察觉市场需求，用有限的成本达到最好的效果，如果我在那时候遇见你，一定会在营销部门给你预留一个位置。
t_en: You could smell out exact market demands and deliver maximum impact with minimal resources. Had we met back then, I would've made sure to secure a spot for you in the marketing department.

t_ch: 呵，偏偏是在这种地方被人承认了，不甘心啊。
t_en: Heh. I'd rather not be recognized for THOSE achievements.

t_ch: 明明可以腰缠万贯，却把所有的钱都投入了电影，去追求心中的艺术。光凭这点，我愿意承认，你是一位值得尊重的导演。
t_en: You could've been rolling in riches, but you poured everything into filmmaking to create something you loved. You're a director worthy of respect for that alone.

t_ch: 但你的所作所为，绝不值得原谅。
t_en: But what you've done is unforgivable.

t_ch: 大明星，你这话简直和当年钟表匠说的一模一样。但我认了，我就是没有才能，拍不出真正的艺术。
t_en: The Watchmaker said almost the same thing to me back then. But like I said, I concede. I have no talent and can't create true art.

t_ch: 人一死，似乎对什么都看得开了。
t_en: I've come to terms with that after dying.

t_ch: 既然如此，你能把老奥帝先生的计划告诉我们了吗？
t_en: Then, could you tell us Old Oti's plan?

t_ch: 如果他真的想要实现愿望，完全可以随意雇佣几个御主参战，像综艺节目那样内定结果。
t_en: If he truly wanted to achieve his wish, he could've easily hired a few Masters to stage a variety show with a rigged outcome.

t_ch: 可他反倒故意找来一群不受控的危险人物，打上一场根本不可能打起来的圣杯战争。
t_en: But he deliberately gathered a group of uncontrollable individuals to participate in a Holy Grail War that might never even happen.

t_ch: 在来之前，奥帝先生给了我一个后备方案：当我落败时，老老实实向诸位认输，并诚邀大家前往大剧院做客。
t_en: Mr. Oti gave me a backup plan before I came here. When I lose, I'm to humbly admit defeat and invite everyone to the Grand Theater.

t_ch: 他和圣杯就在那儿等着各位。从他的口中，你们会得到所有问题的答案。
t_en: Mr. Oti and the Holy Grail will be waiting for you there. You'll hear all the answers from him.

t_ch: 另外，就当是为粉丝提供一点额外的服务吧，在我个人看来，奥帝先生找上各位的原因很简单——单纯是收视率会比较高。
t_en: And you, my fan... this little behind-the-scenes is just for you. I believe the reason Mr. Oti reached out to you all is simple — You'll bring in higher ratings for this show.

t_ch: 你们觉得葛瑞迪的话可信吗？
t_en: Do you trust what Grady said?

t_ch: 纵观匹诺康尼，没有比大剧院更合适节目落幕的地方了——只是，波提欧先生，为什么你看我的眼神有点奇怪？
t_en: There's nowhere in Penacony more fitting for a grand finale than the Grand Theater... Mr. Boothill, why are you looking at me like that?

t_ch: 没想到你这浓眉大眼的，竟然会是那种家伙的粉丝。
t_en: Didn't expect someone like you to be that fudger's fan.

t_ch: 应该说，我御主的审美品味向来表里如一。
t_en: You could say my Master's taste is as tacky as his outfit.

t_ch: 比起御主这种让人不快的等级称谓，我还是更喜欢你刚刚发明的「砂、金、先、生」。
t_en: Instead of calling me "Master," a word that brings back dead class structures, I prefer you address me as Mister Aventurine.

t_ch: 瞧见了吧，红色的哥们儿，这群做生意的都一个样，无论是唾骂还是讽刺，他们统统笑纳，就像子弹打在棉花上。
t_en: See, red guy? Those business crooks are all the same. They take everything you throw at them. Insulting them is like punching a cotton fluff ball, ain't no fun at all.

t_ch: 那我就恭敬不如从命了，砂金先生。
t_en: Then I'll graciously comply, Mr. Aventurine.

t_ch: 剧院的决战，想必，那位奥帝先生已经为我们准备好了战场。占据圣杯的他，只会比葛瑞迪更难缠。
t_en: As for the final showdown at the theater, I'm sure that Mr. Oti has already prepared the battleground for us. With the Holy Grail in his possession, he'll be a far more difficult opponent than Grady.

t_ch: 攻城战和集结的战士，终于有点战争的感觉了。
t_en: A siege and warriors assembled to storm the walls. Now, this is starting to feel like a real war.

t_ch: 我也和你们一起去，音符小姐，无论她现在是敌是友，我都要把她带回来。
t_en: I'm going with you. It doesn't matter whether Miss Note is now friend or foe. I will bring her back.

t_ch: 营救公主行动！
t_en: The princess rescue mission is on!

t_ch: 准备群殴老爷爷。
t_en: Time to gang up on the old man.

t_ch: 让奥帝赔精神损失费。
t_en: Make Oti pay for our emotional damage.

t_ch: 说起来，是不是还有个人来着？就在不远处，他正以悄无声息的脚步，缓缓地逃离此地。
t_en: Come to think of it, wasn't there someone else? Not far away, he is slowly slipping away with hushed steps.

t_ch: 有件事我忘了问——波提欧先生，你知道斯科特是哪个部门的吗？
t_en: Oh, by the way, I think I forgot to ask — Mr. Boothill, do you know which department Skott's from?

t_ch: 砂金露出比恶灵更恶灵的笑容，嘴唇微微翕动，无声地念出了那个部门的名字。星际牛仔发出爽朗的笑声，将指节捏得咯咯作响，狞笑着走向斯科特。
t_en: Aventurine flashed a grin more sinister than the devil's own, his lips barely moving as he silently mouthed the name of that department. The space cowboy let out a hearty laugh, cracking his knuckles with a sharp snap, a wicked smirk spreading across his face as he strode toward Skott.

t_ch: 你听到一阵只会出现在动画里的嚎叫声，抑扬顿挫，又不失情感。你明白，要过上相当长一段时间，斯科特才能从病床上下来了。
t_en: You hear a cry straight out of a cartoon: over-the-top, full of flair, and surprisingly heartfelt. And you just know for a fact that Skott won't be getting out of that hospital bed anytime soon.

t_ch: 但请相信宇宙时代的医疗技术——没有任何一个斯科特，在圣杯战争中受到了不可逆的伤害，尤其是在梦中。
t_en: But have faith in the medical advances of the cosmic age — No Skott was irreversibly harmed in the Holy Grail War. Especially not in a dream.

t_ch: 这下舒坦了，咱们出发吧！
t_en: Now that's more like it. Let's move out!

t_ch: 出发喽！
t_en: Let's move out!

t_ch: 我先歇一会儿。
t_en: Give me a moment.

t_ch: 瞧见我手里的电锯了吗？在Assassin的力量加持下，这两把沾满毒药的电锯，能在瞬间发动三次无敌的斩击！两把电锯就是六次！
t_en: You see these chainsaws in my hands? With Assassin's power, these two poisoned saws can cut three times in the blink of an eye! That's six cuts in one go!

t_ch: 有我的「秘剑•电锯回返」，你们就算插上翅膀，也别想从这儿逃离！
t_en: With my "Concealed Sword: Chainsaw Reversal," you won't escape me even if you turn into a swallow!

t_ch: 我还没有……
t_en: I'm not...

t_ch: 哼…没有尽头的回廊，还有和来时完全不同的房间构造。真是讨厌的结界。
t_en: Hmph... An endless corridor. A layout completely different from before. What an annoying Bounded Field.

t_ch: 如果不能让空间平静下来，恐怕很难走出这里。
t_en: If we can't bring this space to calm, we might never find a way out.

t_ch: 制片人，你这个没用的东西！为什么让你去偷袭两个没什么战斗力的角色你都办不到！
t_en: Producer! You useless piece of junk! I sent you to ambush two mere low-threat characters, how did you still mess that up?

t_ch: 还有这音乐…为什么我的宝具没法压制它？
t_en: And this music... Why can't my Noble Phantasm suppress it?

t_ch: 看来你死了太久，连自己的老本行也忘了？在默片时代，电影也是会有配乐的。
t_en: Looks like you've been dead for too long. Forgot your own trade, huh? Even in the silent film era, movies had soundtracks.

t_ch: 一段被遗忘的历史…对现状并无帮助。
t_en: A forgotten piece of history... is no help to us now.

t_ch: 我有七种方法置你们于死地，七种！
t_en: I've got seven ways to end your lives. Seven!

t_ch: 让你们见识见识，什么叫量变引发质变！
t_en: Let me show you the overwhelming strength in numbers!

t_ch: 斯科特，这波是不是你打得有问题？
t_en: Skott, don't you think you've messed this one up?

t_ch: 说得轻巧，你刚才不也被打得吱哇乱叫？
t_en: Shut it. Weren't you just getting your butt kicked a moment ago?

t_ch: （前面那个是…音符小姐？）
t_en: (That person up ahead... is that Miss Note?)

t_ch: 寂静，寂静得只能听见钟表的齿轮转动，那是恶灵喜悦的呼吸。
t_en: Quiet, so quiet, you can hear the ticking of clockwork gears, the breath of an evil spirit, trembling with glee.

t_ch: 滴答，你们果然还是来了。想要带走最后的知更鸟吧？但你们绝不会成功，所有知更鸟都是我的囊中之物。
t_en: Tick-tock. So you've come, after all. Here to steal the last Robin, are you? You'll never succeed. Every Robin belongs to me.

t_ch: 只要集齐所有的知更鸟，就能召唤知更鸟之神，让我吞噬整个匹诺康尼，成为噩梦之王。
t_en: And once I've gathered them all, I'll summon the God of Robins, swallow all of Penacony whole, and become the King of Nightmares.

t_ch: 新设定太多了，一时不知道从哪儿吐槽……
t_en: Too many new plot twists. I don't even know where to start complaining...

t_ch: 知道吗？对恶魔来说，操弄人的情绪就像弹琴，只要轻轻拨弄，就会像这样——
t_en: Did you know? For a demon, manipulating human emotions is like playing an instrument. Just a light touch, and you get something like this—

t_ch: 葛瑞迪先生，请问您如何看待部分媒体将《外太空六号》列为寰宇百大烂片之首？
t_en: Mr. Grady, how do you respond to certain media calling Outer Space 6 the number one worst film in the galaxy?

t_ch: 滥用特效，剧情一塌糊涂。本该认真制作的服化道，却敷衍了事。
t_en: Over-the-top effects. A storyline in complete shambles. Costumes and props that should've been handled with care rushed and half-baked.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的电影之父？倚老卖老的古董罢了，只会蹭热度的烂片之王。
t_en: The "Father of Penaconian Cinema"? More like a washed-up antique, a flop king who only knows to leech off trends.

t_ch: 空有艺术的追求却没有与之相配的能力…这在影视行业并不少见。
t_en: Artistic ambition without the necessary skills to back it up... A common sight in the film industry.

t_ch: 前面那台机器人，难道就是葛瑞迪说的——最强的仆人？我们还是小心些接近看看吧。
t_en: That robot up ahead, could it be the "strongest Servant" Grady was talking about? Better keep our guard up and approach carefully.

t_ch: 我真像他们说的，是个只会拍血浆烂片的三流导演？不，绝不是这样的！
t_en: Were the critics right all along? Am I really a third-rate director who can only take flops? No! It cannot be!

t_ch: 这次的失败只是意外，我已经掌握诀窍了。下部电影，我会证明自己的！
t_en: This was just a mistake. An accident! I know how to improve now. I... I'll prove myself with my next film!

t_ch: 承认吧，你一辈子都拍不出那样的电影。
t_en: Face it. You'll never be able to make a film like that.

t_ch: 那股柔和的力量，把怪物们全镇住了……
t_en: That gentle force... it stopped every single monster...

t_ch: 要不怎么是艾普瑟隆的大明星呢？
t_en: That's why it's Epsilon's superstar!

t_ch: 电影理论、拍摄技巧…我都学了，为什么还是拍不出那样的作品！
t_en: Film theory, cinematography... I've learned them all! Why can I still not make a film like that!?

t_ch: 钟表匠，梅芙恩，他们凭什么得到比我这个业界先驱更高的声誉！
t_en: The Watchmaker and Maeven... What did they do to deserve more fame than me, the pioneer of the entire industry!?

t_ch: 要是他们没有拍出…如果那些电影是我拍的……
t_en: If only they never made those films... If only those films were... mine.

t_ch: <size=36>要是当初我没有放弃专利就好了。</size>
t_en: <size=36>If only... I never gave up my copyright...</size>

t_ch: 这就是最强的防御绝技，黄金三十重圆环！
t_en: Behold! The ultimate defensive move — Thirty Golden Layers of Rho Aias!

t_ch: 就算纳努克本尊来了，也得扇三十个巴掌才能打穿！
t_en: Even Nanook himself would need thirty slaps to break through!

t_ch: 导演，片酬和保险赔偿记得打我女儿账上…呃啊。
t_en: Director, make sure the paycheck and insurance payout go to my daughter's account... ugh.

t_ch: 不死之躯，瞬间移动，精神控制。
t_en: Immortal body, teleportation, mind control.

t_ch: 在我电影里，你们将无所不能。
t_en: In my film, you'll be gods.

t_ch: 看起来，这场战斗也没我们想的那么简单。
t_en: Seems like this battle's more complicated than we thought.

t_ch: 无妨，只要敌人露出破绽，我一击就能结束战斗。
t_en: It's nothing. As soon as the enemies show a weakness, I'll end this with a single strike.

t_ch: 看到鸽川的景色…学生时代的回忆不自觉地涌上心头了。
t_en: Looking at Dovebrook's scenery... memories from my student days just come flooding back.

t_ch: 姬子的学生时代？
t_en: Himeko's student days?

t_ch: 想听，快给我讲讲！
t_en: Tell me, tell me! I want to hear all about it!

t_ch: 也算不上什么有趣的回忆。那时候鸽川区的帮派众多，混混也满街都是，其中有些人总喜欢来找学生的麻烦。
t_en: They're not exactly interesting memories. Back then, Dovebrook District was teeming with gangs. There were hooligans running around everywhere, and some really loved messing with the students.

t_ch: 我长得比同龄人高大一些，恰好在练习格斗技巧。为了给学弟学妹们讨回公道，我时不时会在街上…和混混们开战。
t_en: I was taller than my peers and happened to be training in martial arts. To stand up for my juniors, I would occasionally... get into scraps with the thugs on the streets.

t_ch: 落进鸽川的人就像拉面里的豆芽一样铺满了水面…那样的情景恍如昨日。
t_en: The people who fell into the Dovebrook river would cover the surface like bean sprouts in noodle soup... It all feels like it was just yesterday.

t_ch: 拉面大师，姬子！
t_en: Himeko, noodle master!

t_ch: 落水的混混，很有画面感！
t_en: A thug falling into the water... what a scene!

t_ch: 有时候是混混落水，有时候却是我自己掉进水里。
t_en: Sometimes the thugs fell in. Other times, I took the plunge myself.

t_ch: 那个时候的我活蹦乱跳的，老师总说我像个「上足了发条不停敲锣打鼓的玩具」，走到哪儿都要闹出些大动静。
t_en: Back then I was a bundle of restless energy. The teachers always said I was like "a wind-up toy that never stopped banging its drums and cymbals," stirring up a commotion wherever I went.

t_ch: 一眨眼，十五年过去了。真是难以想象，这样莽撞任性的我，竟然成了星穹列车的领航员。
t_en: In the blink of an eye, fifteen years have passed. Hard to believe someone as reckless and willful as me became the Navigator of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 眼下不该是伤春悲秋的时间。我们到鸽川边了，「酒馆」的入口藏在哪儿呢？
t_en: Now's not the time to wax sentimental. We've reached the banks of the Dovebrook, so where's the "tavern" entrance hidden?

t_ch: 诸位…诸位听过那个关于鸽川的笑话吗？
t_en: Hey folks... have y'all heard the joke about the Dovebrook?

t_ch: 快给我们讲讲。
t_en: Come on, tell us!

t_ch: 有一天，有个罪人逃出了监狱。他没命地跑啊跑，眼看要逃出生天，却被鸽川拦住了去路。
t_en: One day, a criminal escaped from prison. He ran for his life, but just when freedom seemed within reach, the Dovebrook blocked his path.

t_ch: 眼看身后追兵就要到了，那人便对着鸽川祈祷：「我是个满身污秽的罪人，高洁的流水啊，请不要吞没我。」
t_en: With his pursuers closing in, the man prayed to the river: "I am hammered by the law, stained with filth. O pure, flowing waters, please do not swallow me."

t_ch: 鸽川中有个声音这样回应他道：「水上有个光的台座，踩着它就能过河！」
t_en: A voice from the Dovebrook replied, "There's a pedestal of light upon the water. Step on it, and you can cross!"

t_ch: 罪人哭了：「<color=#dbc291ff>哪有什么光的台座？不过是天上的月亮映在水中的影子罢了，这个影子一碰就碎！</color>踩上去我不就掉到河里淹死了吗？」
t_en: The criminal wept: <color=#dbc291ff>"What pedestal of light? It's only the moon mirrored on the water, an image that shatters at a touch!</color> If I step on it, I'll fall in and drown!"

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你们猜鸽川怎么回答他？
t_en: Hahaha, guess how the Dovebrook answered him?

t_ch: 鸽川这样说道：「你根本没『醉』，不然怎么还能分清月亮与台座？」哈哈哈…哈哈哈哈！
t_en: The Dovebrook said, "You're not 'hammered' at all. Otherwise, how could you still tell the moon from a pedestal?" Hahaha... HAHAHA!

t_ch: 你到底在说什么？
t_en: What are you even talking about?

t_ch: 你是在暗示什么吗？
t_en: What are you implying?

t_ch: 好冷的冷笑话…
t_en: That's such a dry joke...

t_ch: 没、没关系…不必非得请我喝一杯的。只要能博各位一笑就好！哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: I—It's fine... You don't have to buy me a drink. As long as I can make you laugh! Hahahahaha!

t_ch: 这个笑话，似乎意有所指啊。他会是「酒馆」里的愚者吗？
t_en: That joke sounds like a hint. Could he be a Fool from the tavern?

t_ch: 也许找到他说的「易碎的月亮」，就能找到踏进酒馆的道路。
t_en: Maybe, if we find the "fragile moon" he mentioned, we'll find the way into the tavern.

t_ch: 离醉汉远一些。
t_en: Keep your distance from the drunk.

t_ch: 眼下咱们该专注寻找酒馆的入口，闲逛的话之后还有机会。
t_en: Focus on finding the tavern entrance right now. There'll be plenty of time to wander later.

t_ch: 花火的谜语是这么说的…<color=#dbc291ff>无论抬头或是低头，人类凝望天空时，看到的都是同一轮幻月。</color>
t_en: Sparkle's riddle says this... <color=#dbc291ff>Whether looking up or down, they all see the same Phantasmoon when gazing at the sky.</color>

t_ch: 我们已经凝望着天空了，那么接下来……
t_en: We've gazed at the sky already, so next...

t_ch: 该找找幻月在哪。
t_en: We go find where the Phantasmoon is.

t_ch: 从这个角度望去，月亮的倒影就在这儿了，水中的它确实看起来有些奇怪。
t_en: From this angle, the moon's reflection is right here. It does look strange in the water.

t_ch: 如果按照名片说的，愚者的谜题是踏入「酒馆」的钥匙，那这轮月亮应该就是酒馆的门锁了。
t_en: If what the card says is true, and the Fool's riddle is the key to entering the tavern, then this moon must be the door lock.

t_ch: 来吧，{NICKNAME}，让我们凑近些瞧瞧这轮奇怪的「月亮」。
t_en: Come on, {NICKNAME}, let's take a closer look at this strange "moon."

t_ch: 踏进水中倒影。
t_en: Step into the reflection in the water.

t_ch: 再仔细看看？
t_en: Take a closer look?

t_ch: 许多个世代前，鸽川已经是一条流淌众多传说的河。
t_en: Many generations ago, the Dovebrook was already a river flowing with countless legends.

t_ch: 「酒馆」的门扉藏在其中，倒也不奇怪。
t_en: It's no surprise the door to the tavern lies hidden among them.

t_ch: 传说大画师绘世曾在鸽川取水洗笔用于作画。
t_en: Legend has it that the master painter Graphia once drew water from the Dovebrook to wash her paintbrushes.

t_ch: 许多后世的绘师也纷纷效仿，因此这儿也有「绘川」的别名。
t_en: Many later artists followed suit. Hence, its other name, the "Painters' Stream."

t_ch: 按照名片所说，「酒馆」在共愿帮总部附近。
t_en: According to the business card, the "tavern" is near the Synwish Syndicate headquarters.

t_ch: 我们去河对岸看看。
t_en: Let's check the other side of the river.

t_ch: 这月亮的影子，真是一碰就碎…
t_en: The moon's reflection... so fragile, it shatters at a touch...

t_ch: 看上去愚者们把月亮的影子牢牢把守起来了。
t_en: Looks like the Fools are guarding the moon's shadow tight.

t_ch: …找到开关炸开阻碍吧。
t_en: ...Find the switch and blast through the obstruction.

t_ch: 有…有入侵者！
t_en: Th-There's an intruder!

t_ch: 不许靠近！
t_en: Stay back!

t_ch: 打碎「月亮」这把锁，我们离酒馆就更近一步了。
t_en: Shatter the "moon" lock, and we're one step closer to the tavern.

t_ch: 再试一次吧！
t_en: Let's try again!

t_ch: 「月亮」要被打破了，再来一次。
t_en: The "moon" is about to break. Let's try again.

t_ch: 看，「酒馆」的影子，出现了。
t_en: Look, the tavern's shadow has appeared.

t_ch: 看来，顺着眼前的道路，咱们应该就能进入「酒馆」了。
t_en: If we follow this path ahead, we should reach the tavern.

t_ch: 当心！
t_en: Watch out!

t_ch: 我差点忘了，你已经是个独当一面的开拓者了……
t_en: I almost forgot. You're a full-fledged Trailblazer now...

t_ch: 「永恒之地」翁法罗斯……
t_en: Amphoreus, The Eternal Land...

t_ch: 智库中没有这个世界的记录，甚至连名字都没有出现过。
t_en: There is no record of this world in the data bank... Not even a mention of its name.

t_ch: 是啊，黑天鹅小姐不是也说了——难以从外部被观测到，只能被忆庭之镜照映出来的世界——神神秘秘的。
t_en: Yeah, that's what Miss Black Swan said. It's a world hidden away from outside observation, with a presence only revealed by the light from the mirror of the Garden of Recollection. It's mysterious, alright.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}上车才没多久，这趟旅程会不会难度太高了？
t_en: You haven't been with us on the Express very long, {NICKNAME}. Will the journey be too much for you to handle?

t_ch: 不会，非常简单。
t_en: Nah, it's no sweat.

t_ch: 我怕，换别的吧。
t_en: Sounds scary. Can we go somewhere else?

t_ch: 我上车都快两年了。
t_en: I've been on the Express for nearly two years now...

t_ch: 你说什么，哪有那么久啦。
t_en: Huh? Has it been that long?

t_ch: 不必紧张。在今天的我们看来，阿基维利未曾涉足的世界是个罕见的概念。但对于当初的列车，这几乎就是日常，触碰世界的边际本就是「开拓」的含义。
t_en: There's no need to be nervous. I understand that it may be difficult for us today to wrap our heads around the idea that there's a world Akivili didn't venture into, but a journey like that was only routine to the Express Crew back then. After all, to trailblaze is to explore the universe's boundaries.

t_ch: 帕姆还不知道这项提议，也得征询列车长的意见。
t_en: We should consider Pom-Pom's opinion, too. They don't know we're considering Amphoreus as a destination.

t_ch: 我会告诉它的，但帕姆向来不会干涉旅程的目的地，我想最后还是会由我们来决定。
t_en: Sure, I'll take care of it. That said, Pom-Pom usually stays out of discussions about destinations. The final decision will likely still be on us to make.

t_ch: 既然如此，不妨留点时间多考虑下吧。
t_en: In that case, we'd best take the time to think this over.

t_ch: 是啊，就算最后不去那儿，燃料问题也得解决。
t_en: Yeah! Plus, even if we pass Amphoreus over, we've still got a fuel problem to solve.

t_ch: 我也这么觉得。
t_en: That's what I thought, too.

t_ch: 那么，我会先回应黑天鹅小姐，将「永恒之地」列为选项之一。
t_en: Alright, then. For now, I'll let Miss Black Swan know that we're considering The Eternal Land as a destination.

t_ch: 可以投反对票吗？
t_en: Can I vote no?

t_ch: 当然可以。我会先回应黑天鹅小姐，将「永恒之地」列为选项之一，但不作最终结论。
t_en: Of course you can. For now, I'll let Miss Black Swan know that we're considering The Eternal Land as a possible destination, but we're also keeping our options open.

t_ch: 考虑到这趟旅程的特殊性，不妨多做些准备。在正式决定前，我也会尝试从列车的盟友那里打听翁法罗斯的消息。
t_en: Seeing as how this is going to be an unusual adventure, it'd be good for us to take some time out to make preparations for the journey. Meanwhile, I'll see if I can find out more about Amphoreus from our allies before we depart.
